metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 8 L
metadata.notes: Public-web research only; no external communication was made. Employer, breeder, applicant, licensee, agent, and legal-holder roles remain distinct. Later employers and successor organizations are not treated as historical rightsholders without cultivar-level assignment evidence. Profiles whose aggregated records span different employers or owners contain record_split_required in webResearchStatus.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Zhengwang Jiang of the Wuhan Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences (WBG/CAS). The reviewed kiwifruit portfolio is institutional breeding work rather than personally held IP. The Jinyan release paper states that Chinese variety rights were approved in 2009 and that domestic propagation and production were licensed in 2007 to China New Agricultural Science & Technology Co., Ltd.; that license is commercial permission, not an ownership transfer.
profiles.headquarters: Wuhan Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences, No. 201 Jiufeng 1 Road, Wuhan, Hubei, China
profiles.leadershipSummary: Jiang remained affiliated with WBG/CAS in a 2025 publication. WBG's kiwifruit program has an institutional bench; the official Yanchang Wang team page supplies a current program contact and lists cultivar-development work.
profiles.candidateParent: Wuhan Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official current affiliation and official cultivar publications support the institutional relationship; they do not establish that WBG/CAS is the registered holder in every territory.

webResearchSources.label: WBG current kiwifruit team and contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a direct commercial target. Preserve WBG/CAS as the breeder institution, the named 2007 Chinese company as a historical domestic licensee only, and verify each foreign filing's holder independently.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Summaira Riaz at the University of California, Davis. She is a named co-breeder of the five disease-resistant wine-grape cultivars, while the University of California licensing program controls commercial nursery licensing. No source supports personal ownership by Riaz.
profiles.headquarters: Department of Viticulture and Enology, University of California, Davis, 595 Hilgard Lane, Davis, CA 95616, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official department directory lists Riaz as SRA 4 SUPV. The cultivar team includes Andrew Walker, Alan Tenscher, Riaz, and Ninfa Romero, and the university licensing office supplies durable institutional bench and continuity.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official directory proves current employment and the official UC licensing page proves institutional commercialization; cultivar-level registries should still be used for territorial holder names.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat Riaz as an institutional breeder, not the personal rightsholder. Route licensing through the University of California and preserve the full co-breeder team in cultivar evidence.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Ninfa Romero at the University of California, Davis. She is a named co-breeder of the five disease-resistant wine-grape cultivars, while the University of California licensing program controls commercial nursery licensing. No source supports personal ownership by Romero.
profiles.headquarters: Department of Viticulture and Enology, University of California, Davis, 595 Hilgard Lane, Davis, CA 95616, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official department directory lists Romero as SRA 3 SUPV. The cultivar team includes Andrew Walker, Alan Tenscher, Summaira Riaz, and Romero, and the university licensing office supplies durable institutional bench and continuity.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official directory proves current employment and the official UC licensing page proves institutional commercialization; cultivar-level registries should still be used for territorial holder names.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat Romero as an institutional breeder, not the personal rightsholder. Route licensing through the University of California and preserve the full co-breeder team in cultivar evidence.

profiles.ownershipType: independent family-farm founder and strawberry breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Efraim Yosef, founder and CEO of Yosef's Farm. Official Australian PBR material identifies Efraim Yosef personally as titleholder for Peles, and U.S. plant-patent records identify him as inventor for Rotemi, Shaked, and Daniel. The farm affiliation is strong, but no reviewed source supports automatically assigning every personal filing to the farm company.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Yosef founded the breeding program in the early 2000s. The official team page states that Asaf Maysels currently manages the breeding program, providing a named technical successor and operating bench; Ervin Lendler leads international marketing.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official team page proves founder/CEO and breeding-program affiliation; it does not prove a blanket transfer of Efraim Yosef's personally registered cultivar rights.

webResearchSources.label: Yosef's Farm team and breeding continuity
Australian PBR journal identifying Peles titleholder

profiles.webResearchNotes: Independent private target. Keep individual and farm-company fields separate and verify title, agent, and foreign licensee for each cultivar before rollup.

profiles.ownershipType: corporate mango breeder and farm evaluator
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Johann du Preez of Citrogold/Bavaria Fruit Estate. Israel's January 2026 PBR gazette lists Citrogold Pty Ltd as applicant and Johann du Preez as breeder for SUMANGO2 through SUMANGO9, with a Bavaria Fruit Estate operating address. The applications support Citrogold's applicant position, not personal title for du Preez and not yet a granted right.
profiles.headquarters: Citrogold Pty Ltd, Stellenpark Business Park, Jamestown, Stellenbosch 7600, South Africa; breeding/evaluation address in the filings: Bavaria Fruit Estate Farm, Grovedale 239K, Hoedspruit, South Africa
profiles.leadershipSummary: Du Preez was publicly identified as Bavaria Fruit Estate's general manager and mango evaluator. Citrogold is a specialized variety-management platform within the Biogold network, providing commercialization and institutional bench beyond one breeder.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official PBR gazette names Citrogold as applicant and du Preez as breeder; this proves the filing affiliation but should not be converted into granted title until each application matures.

webResearchSources.label: National Mango Board breeding evaluation report

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a standalone person target and roll the pending SUMANGO filings to Citrogold at the applicant level. Do not label du Preez or Bavaria Fruit Estate as legal holder without a granted registry record.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Judith E. Johnson, co-breeder with Harold A. Johnson Jr. of Plant Sciences, Inc. (PSI). The HJ-7 U.S. plant patent names PSI as original and current assignee, and the Australian PBR journal identifies both breeders as PSI employees. Plant Sciences Genetics (PSG) is the later operating genetics platform, but historical HJ rights must remain with the assignee shown in the patent record unless a later recorded assignment is found.
profiles.headquarters: Plant Sciences genetics operations, Watsonville, California, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: The HJ breeding pair was founder-dependent and Harold A. Johnson Jr. died in 2012. PSG's official history describes a later family-led institutional platform under Steve Johnson and Mike Johnson with multiple breeding programs, but it does not establish Judith Johnson's current role or transfer each historical patent.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The patent assignment and Australian PBR record establish the historical employer/assignee relationship. PSG is a later operating successor and must not replace PSI in historical title fields without assignment evidence.

webResearchSources.label: HJ-7 U.S. plant patent and assignments
Australian PBR journal naming PSI and employee breeders
Plant Sciences Genetics history and continuity

profiles.webResearchNotes: The source-backed portfolio is blackberry. A local aggregated apple count was not reproducible from the underlying reviewed records and is not attributed here. Preserve PSI as the historical assignee and treat PSG as a successor contact, not an automatic replacement owner.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Luciano Moser, a breeder in the former CRA-Istituto Sperimentale per la Frutticoltura program. Italian government PBR bulletins name the CRA institute as applicant for the reviewed peach and plum cultivars and list Moser with co-breeders including Antonino Nicotra and Luigi Conte. CREA-OFA is the institutional successor for fruit-research operations, but it is not substituted as the original historical applicant.
profiles.headquarters: Historical program: Istituto Sperimentale per la Frutticoltura, Rome, Italy; successor contact: CREA-OFA, Via della Navicella 2/4, 00184 Rome, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: The source-backed activity is a historical team program, not a current person-led business. CREA states that its current Olive, Fruit and Citrus Crops centre consolidated the former fruit-research centres, providing institutional continuity and a public-research bench.
profiles.primaryContactName: CREA - Research Centre for Olive, Fruit and Citrus Crops
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official PBR bulletins identify the institute as applicant and Moser as breeder. CREA-OFA is documented as the later institutional successor, not the original holder name.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Historical public-institution profile; not an independent target. Keep CRA as the named historical applicant and CREA-OFA only as the successor institution/contact unless a registry supplies a formal assignment.

profiles.ownershipType: private breeding-company co-founder and strategic operator
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Adriana Siboni, co-founder with Secondo Danesi of the New Fruits strawberry breeding centre. Geoplant's official breeding history states that New Fruits is the breeder-rights licensor for the historical NF cultivars. Geoplant is the affiliated nursery and later developed its own genetics; Geoplant's later program is not treated as owner of the earlier New Fruits rights without cultivar-level evidence.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official company history describes a long-running family breeding and nursery operation founded by Danesi and Siboni. New Fruits supplied the historical strawberry program and Geoplant provides the current nursery, licensing route, and operating bench; no individual succession allocation was verified.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official company site supports Siboni's family/business affiliation and the operating contact, but identifies New Fruits, not Geoplant, as licensor for the historical NF strawberry rights.

webResearchSources.label: Geoplant official company history and contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll the reviewed NF cultivars to New Fruits at the rights/licensor level and use Geoplant as the current affiliated commercial contact. Official cultivar pages use registered-symbol styling, but no trademark-owner registry evidence was reviewed, so no trademark ownership is asserted.

profiles.ownershipType: retired public-institution breeder and later independent co-breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact Austrian grape breeder is identified, but the aggregated record spans two legally distinct programs. Roesler and Rathay arose during Mayer's career at the Klosterneuburg federal wine and fruit institute. After retirement she collaborated privately with Georg Weiss on later resistant-grape selections including Stefanie and the Evita/Katharina/Philipp/Sophie group. The legal holder for the later joint selections was not established from an official registry source, so those records must not be assigned to the Klosterneuburg institute.
profiles.headquarters: Historical institutional program: Wiener Strasse 74, 3400 Klosterneuburg, Austria; later private collaboration: Burgenland, Austria
profiles.leadershipSummary: Mayer's public-institution work has continuing bench at the Klosterneuburg federal institute. The post-retirement program was a two-person collaboration with Georg Weiss, and no source-backed successor or current licensing office for those private selections was verified.
profiles.primaryContactName: HBLA and Federal Office for Viticulture and Pomology Klosterneuburg
profiles.candidateParent: HBLA and Federal Office for Viticulture and Pomology Klosterneuburg
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official and industry sources support Mayer's historical institute affiliation for Roesler and Rathay only. They do not support assigning her later private joint-breeding rights to that institution.

webResearchSources.label: Primary winery report on Mayer-Weiss collaboration

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split at minimum into historical Klosterneuburg records and post-retirement Mayer/Weiss records. The latter owner remains unresolved pending an official PBR register or assignment document; do not merge the two groups for scoring.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Charles A. 'Chuck' Leslie of the University of California, Davis walnut improvement program. The Durham patent names Leslie and Gale McGranahan as inventors and The Regents of the University of California as assignee. UC's tree-variety licensing program controls commercial access; no source supports personal ownership by Leslie.
profiles.headquarters: University of California, Davis, One Shields Avenue, Davis, CA 95616, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: The program has transitioned from the McGranahan/Leslie era to a broader team led by Pat J. Brown while Leslie remains a contributor on the 2025 improvement report. The UC breeding and licensing organizations provide durable institutional bench.
profiles.primaryContactName: UC Davis InnovationAccess Plant Variety Licensing
profiles.candidateParent: The Regents of the University of California
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The Durham patent supplies assignee proof and the UC licensing page supplies the current institutional commercialization route.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a direct person target and roll the reviewed walnuts to The Regents/UC Davis, preserving cultivar-level inventors and any territory-specific licensees.

profiles.ownershipType: public-research grape breeder with cross-institution career
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Toshihito Ueno. The aggregated records cross two public employers and two rightsholder groups: NARO holds or assigns the NARO grape portfolio, including Sunny Heart and the Queen Nina/Sun Verde/Grosz Krone program, while official Japanese records identify Yamanashi Prefecture as holder for Monde Briller and related Yamanashi selections. Ueno's move from NARO to Yamanashi does not move the older NARO rights with him.
profiles.headquarters: Current/later program: Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station, 1204 Ezohara, Yamanashi-shi, Yamanashi 405-0043, Japan; historical program: NARO, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Both portfolios are institutional, multi-breeder programs. Ueno's publication history documents the NARO-to-Yamanashi career transition, while NARO and Yamanashi each retain independent research, registration, and licensing bench.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official Yamanashi research and MAFF variety records support the later affiliation and Yamanashi-held cultivars. The candidate parent must apply only to that record group, not to historical NARO cultivars.

webResearchSources.label: Sunny Heart patent naming NARO assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split NARO and Yamanashi records before scoring. Do not infer that a later Yamanashi employment relationship transferred Queen Nina, Sun Verde, Grosz Krone, Sunny Heart, or other NARO-origin rights.

profiles.ownershipType: private fruit-farm owner, company president, and apple breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Yoshinori Nakadaira, representative director of Nakahira Farm Co., Ltd. Australian PBR materials identify Nakadaira personally as applicant/breeder or titleholder for Amaiyume, Nappuru, and PetitGold. The company affiliation and operating farm are verified, but no source supports a blanket transfer of those personal rights to the company.
profiles.headquarters: Nakahira Farm Co., Ltd., 3251 Oshima, Matsukawa-machi, Shimoina-gun, Nagano 399-3304, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Nakadaira is the current representative director of a substantial apple-growing and processing business. Public employment material shows a multi-dozen-person organization and training activity, providing operating bench, but no named ownership successor was verified.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Government and official company materials prove Nakadaira's current executive affiliation; the reviewed PBR records continue to identify the individual as applicant/titleholder.

webResearchSources.label: Australian PBR journal identifying individual title
Japan MAFF award profile for Nakahira Farm

profiles.webResearchNotes: Independent private target. Preserve Yoshinori Nakadaira as the documented personal holder where registries say so and treat Nakahira Farm as the affiliated operating company unless an assignment is produced.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Patricia Silva Ritschel of Embrapa Grape and Wine. Official Embrapa cultivar materials identify the Embrapa unit and Ritschel's breeding team for the BRS grape portfolio. Embrapa, not Ritschel personally, is the development and commercialization institution.
profiles.headquarters: Embrapa Grape and Wine, Rua Livramento 515, Bento Goncalves, Rio Grande do Sul 95701-008, Brazil
profiles.leadershipSummary: Ritschel works within a multi-researcher public breeding program. Embrapa Grape and Wine supplies continuing scientific, testing, and commercialization bench, so the portfolio is not dependent on a personal operating company.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official cultivar and repository records prove Ritschel's affiliation and Embrapa's program role. Territorial registers should still control the exact legal holder wording for each filing.

webResearchSources.label: Embrapa Grape and Wine official contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a standalone commercial target and roll the reviewed BRS cultivars to Embrapa at the institutional level. No trademark-owner registry evidence was reviewed, so BRS names are cultivar/brand evidence only.

profiles.ownershipType: public-research strawberry breeder with joint public/private portfolios
profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Junna Kohori of the Mie Prefecture Agricultural Research Institute. The aggregate spans several holder structures: Mie Prefecture for Mie-origin cultivars such as Kaorino; Mie, Kagawa, NARO, and Chiba jointly for Yotsuboshi; and Mie Prefecture with Miyoshi & Co., Ltd. for MYAGMIE-1. Kohori's shared breeder credit does not collapse these owners into Mie alone.
profiles.headquarters: Mie Prefecture Agricultural Research Institute, 530 Ureshinokawakita-cho, Matsusaka, Mie 515-2316, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: The strawberry work is supported by Mie's vegetable-horticulture and research-strategy teams plus cultivar-specific partner institutions. Yotsuboshi and MYAGMIE-1 demonstrate durable multi-institution bench but also require separate licensing routes.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official Mie pages support Kohori's program affiliation and Mie licensing route. They do not make Mie the sole holder of jointly owned Yotsuboshi or MYAGMIE-1.

webResearchSources.label: MAFF Yotsuboshi registration and joint holders
CFIA MYAGMIE-1 rights and breeder record
Mie official Kaorino licensing and contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split at minimum into Mie-only, four-institution Yotsuboshi, and Mie/Miyoshi joint-right groups before scoring. Keep Kohori as breeder and use each registry's holder list for ownership.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Exact identity resolved as Atsushi Imai of NARO. Citrus records and patents identify Imai as breeder/inventor and NARO as holder or assignee; later NARO technical work places him in pear breeding, including the Hoshimaru/Rinka-era program. The crop-program move is internal to NARO and does not create a second owner.
profiles.headquarters: National Agriculture and Food Research Organization, Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan; historical citrus work at the Kuchinotsu Citrus Research Station, Nagasaki, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Imai moved from NARO's citrus program into later pear work within the same national organization. Both programs are team-based and backed by NARO's institutional breeding, IP-management, and licensing staff.
profiles.candidateParent: National Agriculture and Food Research Organization (NARO)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official PBR, patent, publication, and licensing sources support a continuous NARO affiliation and NARO ownership; cultivar-level records remain controlling for any jointly owned exception.

webResearchSources.label: CFIA Mihaya PBR record naming NARO holder
NARO citrus publication with Imai affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: Suppress as a standalone commercial target and roll the reviewed citrus and pear cultivars to NARO. No record split is needed solely because Imai changed crop programs within the same institution.
